question: Which is the definitive treatment for preeclampsia?

options.text: Magnesium sulfate alone
options.explanation: Magnesium sulfate helps prevent seizures but does not cure preeclampsia.

options.text: Immediate delivery of the fetus and placenta
options.explanation: Delivery of the fetus and placenta is the only definitive treatment for preeclampsia.

options.text: Bed rest and increased oral fluids
options.explanation: Bed rest and fluids may be supportive but do not definitively treat the condition.

options.text: Antibiotic therapy
options.explanation: Antibiotics are not a treatment for preeclampsia unless another infection is present.

question: Which statement about cesarean section is most accurate?

options.text: It is surgery to deliver a baby through the mother's abdomen.
options.explanation: A cesarean section is a surgical delivery in which the baby is removed through an abdominal incision.

options.text: It is always safer than vaginal delivery and has no recovery time.
options.explanation: C-sections are major surgery and usually require longer recovery than vaginal birth.

options.text: It is only performed for elective maternal preference.
options.explanation: Most C-sections are done for medical reasons such as fetal distress or labor complications.

options.text: It prevents any future vaginal birth after healing.
options.explanation: Many women can still have a vaginal birth after a prior C-section.

question: Which statement best defines a premature baby?

options.text: A baby born before 37 completed weeks of pregnancy
options.explanation: Premature birth is defined as delivery before 37 completed weeks of pregnancy.

options.text: A baby born after 40 completed weeks of pregnancy
options.explanation: Birth after 40 weeks is post-term, not premature.

options.text: A baby born with low birth weight only
options.explanation: Low birth weight can occur in preemies but does not define prematurity.

options.text: A baby born with congenital heart disease
options.explanation: Congenital heart disease is a separate condition and does not define prematurity.

options.text: Labor that starts before 37 completed weeks of pregnancy
options.explanation: Preterm labor is defined as labor beginning before 37 completed weeks of gestation.

options.text: Labor that starts after 40 completed weeks of pregnancy
options.explanation: Labor after 40 weeks is post-term, not preterm.

options.text: Any contractions during the first trimester
options.explanation: Contractions in the first trimester are not the definition of labor.

options.text: Labor that begins only after the water breaks
options.explanation: Labor can begin before membrane rupture, so this is not required.

question: Which of the following is a childbirth problem that may require Cesarean delivery?

options.text: Preterm labor before 37 weeks
options.explanation: Preterm labor before 37 completed weeks is a childbirth complication and may require Cesarean delivery.

options.text: Morning sickness in early pregnancy
options.explanation: Morning sickness is a common pregnancy symptom, not a childbirth complication.

options.text: Gestational diabetes controlled by diet
options.explanation: Diet-controlled gestational diabetes is a pregnancy condition, but it is not itself a childbirth problem.

options.text: Braxton Hicks contractions at term
options.explanation: Braxton Hicks contractions are false labor and do not indicate a childbirth complication.
